pullRequest.title: docs: mobile responsive panel and header UX implementation spec

nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Unify compact-mode breakpoint semantics to a single exact rule.**

The spec mixes `viewport < md` and `(max-width: 767px)`. Please define one canonical threshold expression (including inclusive/exclusive behavior) and reuse it everywhere to prevent 1px divergence across implementations.
 


Also applies to: 95-96

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/requirements/mobile-responsive-panel-and-header-spec.md` around lines 71
- 72, Unify the compact-mode breakpoint by replacing the mixed expressions
"viewport < md" and "(max-width: 767px)" with one canonical rule (e.g.,
"viewport < 768px" meaning widths strictly less than 768px, or if you prefer
inclusive semantics use "viewport <= 767px" — pick one and state it explicitly)
and update every occurrence (including the instances currently written as
"viewport < md" and "(max-width: 767px)" and the other occurrences mentioned
around lines 95-96) so the spec uses the exact same threshold expression and
inclusive/exclusive wording everywhere.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commits b7fee25 to e21b96d

_🛠️ Refactor suggestion_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Make hook ownership explicit instead of optional.**

`useCompactHeaderMode` location is currently “`packages/epics` or `packages/ui` depending ownership,” which weakens implementation readiness. Pick one canonical module/package in this spec so follow-up work doesn’t fork architecture decisions mid-implementation.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@docs/requirements/mobile-responsive-panel-and-header-spec.md` around lines 88
- 89, The spec should pick a single canonical owner for the hook instead of
leaving it optional: create and document useCompactHeaderMode as a client hook
owned by the packages/ui package (not packages/epics). Update the spec to state
that useCompactHeaderMode lives in packages/ui, implement and export the hook
from that package (ensure exported symbol useCompactHeaderMode and any related
types/props are declared there), and update any references in the spec and
integration notes to point to packages/ui so follow-up work does not split
ownership.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:triton:hawk -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commits b7fee25 to e21b96d

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Distinguish `AiPanelTrigger` accessible labels from `AiSidebarTrigger`.**

Both triggers reuse `t('openPanel')` / `t('closePanel')` from the same `AiPanel` namespace (lines 142-143 vs 174-175), but they now control different surfaces: `AiSidebarTrigger` toggles the compact overlay menu (Menu/PanelLeftClose icons) while `AiPanelTrigger` toggles the AI panel proper (Sparkles icon). Screen reader users encountering both controls in the header will hear the same announcement for two distinct affordances.

Consider dedicated keys (e.g. `t('openAiPanel')` / `t('closeAiPanel')` for the Sparkles trigger, keeping the overlay menu wording for `AiSidebarTrigger`).

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/epics/src/common/panel-wrap-layout.tsx` around lines 154 - 180,
AiPanelTrigger currently reuses the generic AiPanel translation keys that are
also used by AiSidebarTrigger, causing duplicate accessible labels; update
AiPanelTrigger to use dedicated translation keys (e.g. t('openAiPanel') and
t('closeAiPanel')) for the Sparkles button's title and aria-label, and add
corresponding keys to the AiPanel translations, leaving AiSidebarTrigger's
existing keys for the overlay menu unchanged; adjust any tests or story text
that reference the old keys if present.
```

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commits b7fee25 to e21b96d

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Hydration mismatch from `viewportWidth` initialization.**

`PanelWrapLayout` is a client component, so it still server-renders to HTML. The lazy initializer returns `1440` on the server and `window.innerWidth` on the client's first render, before any `useEffect` runs. Downstream values that flow into rendered markup — most directly `rightSidebarWidth` (line 309-311), which is written into `style={{ '--sidebar-width': rightSidebarWidth }}` on the `SidebarProvider` at line 465 — will therefore differ between SSR output and client hydration whenever the actual viewport is below `DUAL_PANEL_MIN_VIEWPORT_PX`. On narrow viewports this produces a React hydration warning and a flash of incorrect sizing before the resize effect corrects it.

Initialize to a deterministic SSR-stable value and update from `window.innerWidth` in the existing `useEffect`:

<details>
<summary>🛠️ Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
-  const [viewportWidth, setViewportWidth] = useState<number>(() => {
-    if (typeof window === 'undefined') return 1440;
-    return window.innerWidth;
-  });
+  // Start with the dual-panel threshold so SSR and the first client render agree;
+  // the resize effect below reads the real viewport on mount.
+  const [viewportWidth, setViewportWidth] = useState<number>(
+    DUAL_PANEL_MIN_VIEWPORT_PX,
+  );
```

The `onResize()` call inside the resize effect (line 326) already syncs to the real width on mount.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/epics/src/common/panel-wrap-layout.tsx` around lines 294 - 311, The
SSR/client hydration mismatch comes from initializing viewportWidth to
window.innerWidth on first client render; change the useState initializer in
PanelWrapLayout (viewportWidth) to a deterministic SSR-stable value (e.g.
DUAL_PANEL_MIN_VIEWPORT_PX) instead of 1440 or window.innerWidth, and rely on
the existing resize effect's onResize call to update viewportWidth on mount so
rightSidebarWidth and downstream style (--sidebar-width) are consistent between
SSR and hydration.
```

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commits b7fee25 to 13b2f61

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Add a fallback for `sellerLabel` in banner interpolation.**

`sellerLabel` can be undefined here, which degrades the new localized body text. Please pass a guaranteed string fallback before calling `t(...)`.

 

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-  const sellerLabel = counterpartyLabel;
+  const sellerLabel = counterpartyLabel || t('exchangeCounterpartyFallback');
```

```diff
+    "exchangeCounterpartyFallback": "the other party",
```

```diff
+    "exchangeCounterpartyFallback": "die andere Partei",
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/epics/src/spaces/components/space-escrow-deposit-banner.tsx` around
lines 276 - 286, The banner interpolation calls t(...) with sellerLabel which
can be undefined; update both places where t('acceptInvestmentBody', {
sellerLabel, ... }) and t('exchangeStakesAndTokensBody', { sellerLabel, ... })
are used in space-escrow-deposit-banner.tsx to pass a guaranteed string fallback
for sellerLabel (e.g., use a default like an empty string or a localized
fallback) before invoking t so the translated body always receives a string.
```

</details>

✅ Addressed in commits b7fee25 to 13b2f61

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Internationalize the close label.**

The `closeLabel` is hardcoded as `"Close"` instead of using a translation key. This violates the i18n requirement that all user-facing text should use translation keys.




<details>
<summary>🌍 Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
           <SheetContent
             side="right"
-            closeLabel="Close"
+            closeLabel={t('close')}
             className={cn(
```

</details>

As per coding guidelines: "i18n: all user-facing text uses translation keys"

```suggestion
             side="right"
             closeLabel={t('close')}
             className={cn(
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_end -->

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@packages/epics/src/people/components/button-profile.tsx` at line 129, Replace
the hardcoded closeLabel="Close" in the ButtonProfile component with a
translation key (e.g. closeLabel={t('people.buttonProfile.close')}), import and
use the existing i18n helper (useTranslation or t) inside the component to get
the translated string, and add the corresponding key
("people.buttonProfile.close") to the translation resource files/locales; update
any prop types if necessary to accept string from t().
```

</details>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Addressed in commits b7fee25 to 13b2f61

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Invisible desktop profile still consumes mobile header space.**

`max-md:pointer-events-none max-md:opacity-0` hides interaction/visuals but keeps layout width, which can still cause narrow-header collisions. Use `max-md:hidden` so the desktop profile is removed from mobile layout entirely.  
 

<details>
<summary>Proposed fix</summary>

```diff
- <div className="flex max-md:pointer-events-none max-md:opacity-0">
+ <div className="flex max-md:hidden">
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx` around lines 324 - 348, The desktop profile
wrapper div currently uses classes "max-md:pointer-events-none max-md:opacity-0"
which hides it visually but still reserves layout space on small screens; change
the wrapper around ConnectedButtonProfile to use "max-md:hidden" so the element
is removed from the mobile layout entirely (locate the div containing
ConnectedButtonProfile in layout.tsx and replace those responsive classes with
max-md:hidden), keeping the same props passed to ConnectedButtonProfile and
retaining trailingBeforeProfile logic.
```

</details>
